## [00:00 - 03:03]

So in this last slide, I just want to give you a big picture overview of the pre-launch sequence. So, you have seen this over and over throughout Product Launch Formula, the old sideways sales letter. This is the three pieces of pre-launch content. And it's really easy, again, on the outside looking into one of these launches to think that's all there is. It's just simply three pieces of pre-launch content, three videos that lead into the open cart, lead into the sales video. Right? So, it's really easy to think that's all there is. But I want you to think globally around this whole process because there's a lot more going on and one of the big things is email. So back here, you have your email list, right? And you actually have a squeeze page that leads into your email list. And you have traffic, the different types of traffic that lead into your squeeze page that give people a lead magnet, which is usually your pre-launch content. And then this is your list. So you have an email that goes out and says, go check out this first video. And then when the second piece of PLC or the second video comes out, you have another email that says, go check that out. And then when the third one comes out, you have another email that goes out and says, go check out this piece of, this great video, this great piece of content. So that's the basics. But it goes beyond that because generally what we'll do is if people don't click through, if they don't open this email and click through and go check that out, then you have another email that goes to that. And guess what? One of the biggest predictors of how well launch is going to do is the engagement with this first piece of pre-launch content. So you really want to focus on getting that first piece of pre-launch content consumed. And then you might even have another email and you have to judge this. There is some judgment involved in here and we'll go over that in the future in looking at open rates and how they're doing and click rates and the engagement. But you've got an email that might be coming out here that says, hey, just make sure you saw video one because we've got video two coming out tomorrow. So there could be, it's possible to send too many emails, but you also don't want to air in the side of sending too few emails. Another one, maybe to the people that haven't engaged in PLC2, you send out, hey, did you see this video? And again, here, go into there. And then guess what? After this third video, you've got emails, you've got a whole sequence of emails going

## [03:03 - 04:26]

during your open card sequence. This isn't really part of pre-launch, but you have a whole sequence there going in. Then also down below, in each one of these videos, you have these comment sections, comments under your videos. And you can be in here answering comments, answering objections, getting people stoked, getting them fired up. So this entire sideways sales audit, really the entire pre-launch sequence is more than just releasing these videos. And I want you to think conceptually around this entire thing. There could even be another layer here, which is social, social media. If you have a significant social media following, you're putting social posts in here that are driving people up into your pre-launch content. So this video is just sort of an overview of the whole thing. We're going to be walking through this in this module. But I want you to be thinking globally about the people that are going through this process aren't just getting three videos. They're getting three videos plus emails, plus they're looking at comments, and they might even be looking at social media. Very important to think about the big picture, and it's not just you're putting out three videos.
